feat->Wizard Spellcasting.name: Wizard Spellcasting
feat->Wizard Spellcasting.description:
Through dedicated study and practice, you can construct spells with academic rigor by shaping arcane magic. You are a spellcaster, and you can cast spells of the arcane tradition using the Cast a Spell activity. As a wizard, when you cast spells, your incantations likely specify exactly what forces you call on and how to shape them, and your gestures precisely shape and direct your magic while circles of arcane runes flare to life.

At 1st level, you can prepare up to two 1st-rank spells and five cantrips each morning from the spells in your spellbook (see below), as well as one extra curriculum cantrip and one extra curriculum spell of each rank you can cast from your arcane school. Prepared spells remain available to you until you cast them or until you prepare your spells again. The number of spells you can prepare is called your spell slots.

As you increase in level as a wizard, the number of spells you can prepare each day increases, as does the highest rank of spell you can cast, as shown in the Wizard Spells per Day table.

Some of your spells require you to attempt a spell attack to see how effective they are, or have your enemies roll against your spell DC (typically by attempting a saving throw). Since your key attribute is Intelligence, your spell attack modifier and spell DC use your Intelligence modifier. Details on calculating these statistics appear on page 403.

Heightening Spells

When you get spell slots of 2nd rank and higher, you can fill those slots with stronger versions of lower-rank spells. This increases the spell's rank, heightening it to match the spell slot. Many spells have specific improvements when they are heightened to certain ranks.

Cantrips

Some of your spells are cantrips. A cantrip is a special type of spell that doesn't use spell slots. You can cast a cantrip at will, any number of times per day. A cantrip is always automatically heightened to half your level rounded up—this is usually equal to the highest rank of wizard spell slot you have. For example, as a 1st-level wizard, your cantrips are 1st-rank spells, and as a 5th-level wizard, your cantrips are 3rd-rank spells.

Spellbook

Every arcane spell has a written version, which you record in your personalized book of spells. You start with a spellbook worth 10 sp or less, which you receive for free and must study each day to prepare your spells. Your spellbook's form and name are up to you. It might be a musty, leather-bound tome or an assortment of thin metal disks connected to a brass ring; its name might be esoteric, like The Tome of Silent Shadows or something more academic, like Advanced Pyromantic Applications of Jalmeri Elemental Theory.

The spellbook contains your choice of 10 arcane cantrips and five 1st-rank arcane spells. You choose these from the common spells on the arcane spell list or from other arcane spells you gain access to. You also add two 1st-rank spells from the curriculum of your arcane school (except in the case of the school of unified magical theory, as described in that school).

Each time you gain a level, you add two arcane spells to your spellbook, of any spell rank for which you have spell slots, chosen from common spells of your tradition or others you gain access to and learn via Learn a Spell. When you gain spell slots of a new rank, you also add an additional spell from your school's curriculum (unless it's the school of unified magical theory).

feat->Arcane Thesis.name: Arcane Thesis
feat->Arcane Thesis.description: During your studies to become a full-fledged wizard, you produced a thesis of unique magical research. You gain a special benefit depending on the topic of your thesis research. The arcane thesis topics presented in this book are below; your specific thesis probably has a much longer and more technical title like "On the Methods of Spell Interpolation and the Genesis of a New Understanding of the Building Blocks of Magic."

feat->Arcane School.name: Arcane School
feat->Arcane School.description:
Most wizards acquire their knowledge of spells from a formal educational institution, such as the Arcanamirium or the Magaambya. At 1st level, you choose your arcane school, which grants you magical abilities.

You gain additional spells and spell slots from the curriculum taught at the school you attended. Arcane schools are described in detail on page 198. Some wizards follow the school of unified magical theory, which attempts to forge a new school by studying independently and drawing information from a multitude of texts and tutors. Though a wizard with this approach lacks the focus of formal training, they have greater flexibility.

feat->Arcane Bond.name: Arcane Bond
feat->Arcane Bond.description: You place some of your magical power in a bonded item. Each day when you prepare your spells, you can designate a single item you own as your bonded item. This is typically an item associated with spellcasting, such as a wand, ring, or staff, but you are free to designate a weapon or other item. You gain the @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.v82XtjAVN4ffgVVR]{Drain Bonded Item} free action.

action->Cast a Spell.description:
tag will get replaced with text from compendium entry @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.aBQ8ajvEBByv45yz]

You cast a spell you have prepared or in your repertoire. Casting a Spell is a special activity that takes a variable number of actions depending on the spell, as listed in each spell's stat block. As soon as the spellcasting actions are complete, the spell effect occurs.

Some spells are cast as a reaction or free action. In those cases, you Cast the Spell as a reaction or free action (as appropriate) instead of as an activity. Such cases will be noted in the spell's stat block-for example, "[reaction] verbal."

Long Casting Times Some spells take minutes or hours to cast. The Cast a Spell activity for these spells includes a mix of the listed spell components, but it's not necessary to break down which one you're providing at a given time. You can't use other actions or reactions while casting such a spell, though at the GM's discretion, you might be able to speak a few sentences. As with other activities that take a long time, these spells have the exploration trait, and you can't cast them in an encounter. If combat breaks out while you're casting one, your spell is disrupted.

Spell Components Each spell lists the spell components required to cast it after the action icons or text, such as "[three-actions] material, somatic, verbal." The spell components, described in detail below, add traits and requirements to the Cast a Spell activity. If you can't provide the components, you fail to Cast the Spell.

  • Material (manipulate)
  • Somatic (manipulate)
  • Verbal (concentrate)
  • Focus (manipulate)

Disrupted and Lost Spells Some abilities and spells can disrupt a spell, causing it to have no effect and be lost. When you lose a spell, you've already expended the spell slot, spent the spell's costs and actions, and used the Cast a Spell activity. If a spell is disrupted during a @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.3f5DMFu8fPiqHpRg]{Sustain a Spell} action, the spell immediately ends.

feat->Spell Substitution.name: Spell Substitution
feat->Spell Substitution.description:
You don't accept the fact that once spells are prepared, they can't be changed until your next daily preparations, and you have uncovered a shortcut allowing you to substitute new spells for those you originally prepared.

You can spend 10 minutes to empty one of your prepared spell slots and prepare a different spell from your spellbook in its place. If you are interrupted during such a swap, the original spell remains prepared and can still be cast. You can try again to swap out the spell later, but you must start the process over again.

feat->School of Battle Magic.name: School of Battle Magic
feat->School of Battle Magic.description:
Magic is power, and there are always those who will use power for the art of battle. You may have studied in the military of a nation of the brink of war—Cheliax and Andoran perhaps, or Geb and Nex. You summon whirling energies that can lay waste to both soldiers and armies, while being sure not to neglect arcane countermeasures for common tactical complications or the shields and defenses that keep combatants alive on the battlefield.

Curriculum

  • cantrips: @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.TVKNbcgTee19PXZR]{Shield}, @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.60sgbuMWN0268dB7]{Telekinetic Projectile}
  • 1st: @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.y6rAdMK6EFlV6U0t]{Breathe Fire}, @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.gKKqvLohtrSJj3BM]{Force Barrage}, @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.aAbfKn8maGjJjk2W]{Mystic Armor}
  • 2nd: @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.9XHmC2JgTUIQ1CCm]{Mist}, @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Fr58LDSrbndgld9n]{Resist Energy}
  • 3rd: @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.gPvtmKMRpg9I9D7H]{Earthbind}, @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.sxQZ6yqTn0czJxVd]{Fireball}
  • 4th: @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.IarZrgCeaiUqOuRu]{Wall of Fire}, @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.8M03UxGXjYyDFAoy]{Weapon Storm}
  • 5th: @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.xxWhyl81w3ckslAU]{Howling Blizzard}, @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.oXeEbcUdgJGWHGEJ]{Impaling Spike}
  • 6th: @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.TDNlDWbYb58Y55Da]{Chain Lightning}, @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.r7ihOgKv19eJQnik]{Disintegrate}
  • 7th: @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.m2xFMNyQiUKQDRaj]{Energy Aegis}, @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.AlbpWWN87yGegoAF]{True Target}
  • 8th: @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.C2GYCH3TtUFqPfdX]{Arctic Rift}, @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.M0jQlpQYUr0pp2Sv]{Desiccate}
  • 9th: @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.jrBa9deU2ULFWvSl]{Falling Stars}

School Spells initial: @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Hu38hoAUSYeFpkVa]{Force Bolt}; advanced: @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.LoBjvguamA12iyW0]{Energy Absorption}

consumable->Owlbear Claw.description:

Uso fijado a un arma

Activar F Visualizar

Desencadenante Tienes un éxito crítico en una tirada de ataque con el arma fijada

Esta garra engarzada en un broche de hierro y una cadena no siempre es la garra de un oso búho. Cuando activas elgarra, el ataque desencadenante obtiene el efecto de especialización crítica del arma.

feat->Muses.name: Muses
feat->Muses.description:
As a bard, you select one muse at 1st level. This muse leads you to great things, and might be a physical creature, a deity, a philosophy, or a captivating mystery.

  • @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.classfeatures.Item.4ripp6EfdVpS0d60]{Enigma}
  • @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.classfeatures.Item.YMBsi4bndRAk5CX4]{Maestro}
  • @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.classfeatures.Item.y0jGimYdMGDJWrEq]{Polymath}
  • @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.classfeatures.Item.N03BtRvjX9TeHRa4]{Warrior}

feat->Composition Spells.name: Composition Spells
feat->Composition Spells.description:
Composition Spells

You can infuse your performances with magic to create unique effects called compositions. Compositions are a special type of spell that often require you to use the Performance skill when casting them. Composition spells are a type of focus spell. It costs 1 Focus Point to cast a focus spell, and you start with a focus pool of 1 Focus Point. You refill your focus pool during your daily preparations, and you can regain 1 Focus Point by spending 10 minutes using the Refocus activity to perform, write a new composition, or otherwise engage your muse.

Focus spells are automatically heightened to half your level rounded up, much like cantrips. Focus spells don't require spell slots, and you can't cast them using spell slots. Taking feats can give you more focus spells and increase the size of your focus pool, though your focus pool can never hold more than 3 Focus Points.

You learn the @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.WILXkjU5Yq3yw10r]{Counter Performance} composition spell, protecting against auditory and visual effects.

Composition Cantrips

Composition cantrips are special composition spells that don't cost Focus Points, so you can use them as often as you like. Composition cantrips are in addition to the cantrips you choose from the occult list. Generally, only feats can give you more composition cantrips. Unlike other cantrips, you can't swap out composition cantrips gained from bard feats at a later level, unless you swap out the specific feat via retraining. You learn the @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.IAjvwqgiDr3qGYxY]{Courageous Anthem} composition cantrip, which boosts your allies' attacks, damage, and defense against fear.

feat->Occult Spellcasting.name: Occult Spellcasting
feat->Occult Spellcasting.description:
You draw upon magic from esoteric knowledge. You can cast occult spells using the Cast a Spell activity, and you can supply material, somatic, and verbal components when casting spells. Because you're a bard, you can usually play an instrument for spells requiring somatic or material components, as long as it takes at least one of your hands to do so. If you use an instrument, you don't need a material component pouch or another hand free. You can usually also play an instrument for spells requiring verbal components, instead of speaking.

Each day, you can cast up to two 1st-rank spells. You must know spells to cast them, and you learn them via the spell repertoire class feature. The number of spells you can cast each day is called your spell slots.

As you increase in level as a bard, your number of spells per day increases, as does the highest rank of spells you can cast.

Some of your spells require you to attempt a spell attack roll to see how effective they are, or have your enemies roll against your spell DC (typically by attempting a saving throw). Since your key ability is Charisma, your spell attack rolls and spell DCs use your Charisma modifier.

feat->Spell Repertoire (Bard).name: Spell Repertoire (Bard)
feat->Spell Repertoire (Bard).description:
The collection of spells you can cast is called your spell repertoire. At 1st level, you learn two 1st-rank spells of your choice and five cantrips of your choice. You choose these from the common spells from the occult spell list, or from other occult spells to which you have access. You can cast any spell in your spell repertoire by using a spell slot of an appropriate spell rank.

You add to this spell repertoire as you increase in level. Each time you get a spell slot, you add a spell of the same rank to your spell repertoire. At 2nd level, you select another 1st-rank spell; at 3rd level, you select two 2nd-rank spells, and so on. When you add spells, you might choose a higher-rank version of a spell you already have so that you can cast a heightened version of that spell.

Though you gain them at the same rate, your spell slots and the spells in your spell repertoire are separate. If a feat or other ability adds a spell to your spell repertoire, it wouldn't give you another spell slot, and vice versa.

Swapping Spells in your Repertoire

As you gain new spells in your spell repertoire, you might want to replace some of the spells you previously learned. Each time you gain a level and learn new spells, you can swap out one of your old spells for a different spell of the same rank. This spell can be a cantrip. You can also swap out spells by retraining during downtime.

feat->Lightning Reflexes.name:

Reflejos relámpago
feat->Lightning Reflexes.description: Your reflexes are lightning fast. Your proficiency rank for Reflex saves increases to expert.

feat->Signature Spells.name: Signature Spells
feat->Signature Spells.description:
Experience enables you to cast some spells more flexibly.

For each spell rank you have access to, choose one spell of that rank to be a signature spell. You don't need to learn heightened versions of signature spells separately; instead, you can heighten these spells freely. If you've learned a signature spell at a higher level than its minimum, you can also cast all its lower-rank versions without learning those separately. If you swap out a signature spell, you can replace it with any spell you could have chosen when you first selected it (i.e., of the same spell rank or lower).

You can also retrain specifically to change a signature spell to a different spell of that rank without swapping any spells; this takes as much time as retraining a spell normally does.

feat->Polymath.name: Polymath
feat->Polymath.description: Your muse is a jack of all trades, flitting between skills and pursuits. If it's is a creature, it might be an eclectic creature like a fey; if a deity, it might be Desna or Calistria. As a bard with a polymath muse, you are interested in a wide array of topics but rarely dedicated to any one, and you rarely make up your mind-you want to try everything. You gain the @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.feats-srd.Item.jBp91q4uzwd4FeSX]{Versatile Performance} feat and add @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.xqmHD8JIjak15lRk]{Phantasmal Minion} to your spell repertoire.

feat->Plant Nourishment.name: Plant Nourishment
feat->Plant Nourishment.description: You gain nourishment in the same way that the plants or fungi that match your body type normally do, through some combination of photosynthesis, absorbing minerals with your roots, or scavenging decaying matter. You typically do not need to pay for food. If you normally rely on photosynthesis and go without sunlight for 1 week, you begin to starve. You can derive nourishment from specially formulated bottles of sunlight instead of natural sunlight, but these bottles cost 10 times as much as standard rations (or 40 sp).

Grimmnir.name: Grimmnir
Grimmnir.appearance:
Grimmnir es un mago dragón, un vástago de la magia dracónica a quien casi todos los kobolds respetan y honran. Al menos eso es lo que afirma Grimmnir. Otros Hookclaws han notado que su magia no se parece mucho a la de otros magos dragones kobold. En pocas palabras, Grimmnir es... espeluznante. Donde los dragones palpitan con poder arcano y carisma ilimitado, la hechicería de Grimmnir siempre se ha sentido premonitoria y odiosa, mezclada con un escalofrío de condenación que trepa por la columna vertebral y muerde la espalda.de la mente. Sin embargo, Grimmnir siempre da mucho miedo y sus ojos brillan cada vez que alguien menciona el tema, por lo que los Hookclaws mantienen la boca cerrada y no discuten con ella.

Grimmnir les presta poca atención. Ella sabe que es una verdadera hija de dragones. Desde que era una cría, Grimmnir ha soñado con ellos: wyrms que acaban con el mundo y que acechan en los rincones más profundos de la realidad, debajo de las olas y más allá del universo, que roen las raíces de la existencia y se elevarán.desde las profundidades al final de los días. Sin embargo, aunque ese poder destructivo es su legado, Grimmnir ha decidido no seguir los pasos de esos grandes wyrms, no del todo. El kobold ha realizado una cuidadosa consideración de todos los pros y los contras, y ha decidido que destruir el mundo sería personalmente inconveniente y más problemático de lo que vale la pena.

De todos modos, Grimmnir se siente obligada a aceptar su destino de convertirse en una de las devoradoras de mundos,incluso si ella nunca devora el mundo. En su opinión, esto significa crecer mucho, mucho más y desarrollar su fuerza por cualquier medio posible. A pesar de atiborrarse de comida y otras ideas brillantes, la kobold no ha tenido suerte aumentando su altura, por lo que se ha visto obligada a volcar todos sus esfuerzos en la superación personal. Con este fin, Grimmnir se ha unido a la milicia kobold, con la intención de utilizar sus garras con puntas ácidas y su temible mordisco contra cualquier obstáculo que encuentre. ElLos Hookclaws aprecian su destreza para proteger a la tribu, ¡y lo aprecian aún más cuando Grimmnir está patrullando y, por lo tanto, no se le ve por ningún lado!

consumable->Scroll of Ray of Enfeeblemen.name:
La etiqueta <Compendium> se reemplazará con el texto de la entrada del compendio @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.equipment-srd.Item.RjuupS9xyXDLgyIr]

consumable->Scroll of Ray of Enfeeblemen.consumableSpellDescription:
A ray that saps a foe's strength flashes from your hand. Attempt a ranged spell attack against the target. If you succeed, that creature attempts a Fortitude save to determine the spell's effect. If your attack roll is a critical success, use the outcome for one degree of success worse than the result of its save.

Critical Success The target is unaffected.

Success The target becomes @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.MIRkyAjyBeXivMa7]{Debilitado 1}.

Failure The target becomes @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.MIRkyAjyBeXivMa7]{Debilitado 2}.

Critical Failure The target becomes @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.MIRkyAjyBeXivMa7]{Debilitado 3}.
consumable->Scroll of Ray of Enfeeblemen.description:
La etiqueta se reemplazará con el texto de la entrada del compendio @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.equipment-srd.Item.RjuupS9xyXDLgyIr]

@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.J7Y7tl0bbdz7TcCc]{Enfeeble}

Lanzar un hechizo desde un pergamino requiere sostener el pergamino en una mano y activarlo con una actividad de Lanzar un hechizo usando el número normal de acciones para ese hechizo.

Para lanzar un hechizo desde un pergamino, el hechizo debe aparecer en tu lista de hechizos. Como eres tú quien lanza el hechizo, usa tu tirada de ataque de hechizo ydeletrea DC. El hechizo también obtiene el rasgo apropiado para tu tradición (arcana, divina, oculta o primordial).

Todos los componentes materiales físicos y los costos se proporcionan cuando se crea un pergamino, por lo que no es necesario proporcionarlos al lanzar un hechizo a partir de un pergamino. Debes reemplazar cualquier componente material requerido para ese hechizo con un componente somático. Si el hechizo requiere un enfoque, debes tener ese enfoque para lanzar el hechizo desde un pergamino.

Nota: Para crear un pergamino o varita de un hechizo específico, arrastre el hechizo desde el compendio o navegador de compendio al inventario de un PC, NPC o actor de botín.

You can't replace lost pieces or repair an object that's been completely destroyed.

Heightened (2nd) You can target a non-magical object of 1 Bulk or less.

Heightened (3rd) You can target a non-magical object of 2 Bulk or less, or a magical object of 1 Bulk or less.

feat->Draconic Exemplar (Black Dragon).name:
La etiqueta <Compendium> se reemplazará con el texto de la entrada del compendio @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.ancestryfeatures.Item.egpiSWBrNBb1Fmig]
Ejemplar dracónico (Dragón Negro)
feat->Draconic Exemplar (Black Dragon).description:
You draw minor powers from your draconic exemplar. Choose a type of chromatic or metallic dragon to be your exemplar. This determines your scale color and appearance, and dragons sometimes look more favorably upon those kobolds who resemble them, at the GM's discretion. Your exemplar may also determine details of other abilities you have, using the Draconic Exemplars table.

feat->Spell Repertoire (Sorcerer).name: Spell Repertoire (Sorcerer)
feat->Spell Repertoire (Sorcerer).description:
The collection of spells you can cast is called your spell repertoire. At 1st level, you learn two 1st-rank spells of your choice and four cantrips of your choice, as well as an additional spell and cantrip from your bloodline. You choose these from the common spells from the tradition corresponding to your bloodline, or from other spells from that tradition to which you have access. You can cast any spell in your spell repertoire by using a spell slot of an appropriate spell rank.

You add to this spell repertoire as you increase in level. Each time you get a spell slot, you add a spell of the same rank to your spell repertoire. At 2nd level, you select another 1st-rank spell; at 3rd level, you select two 2nd-rank spells, and so on. When you add spells, you might choose a higher-rank version of a spell you already have so that you can cast a heightened version of that spell.

Though you gain them at the same rate, your spell slots and the spells in your spell repertoire are separate. If a feat or other ability adds a spell to your spell repertoire, it wouldn't give you another spell slot, and vice versa.

Swapping Spells in your Repertoire

As you gain new spells in your spell repertoire, you might want to replace some of the spells you previously learned. Each time you gain a level and learn new spells, you can swap out one of your old spells for a different spell of the same rank. This spell can be a cantrip. You can also swap out spells by retraining during downtime.

feat->Sorcerer Spellcasting.name: Sorcerer Spellcasting
feat->Sorcerer Spellcasting.description:
Sorcerer Spellcasting

Your bloodline provides you with incredible magical power. You can cast spells using the Cast a Spell activity, and you can supply material, somatic, and verbal components when casting spells. Because you're a sorcerer, you can usually replace material components with somatic components, so you don't need to use a spell component pouch.

Each day, you can cast up to three 1st-rank spells. You must know spells to cast them, and you learn them via the spell repertoire class feature. The number of spells you can cast each day is called your spell slots.

As you increase in level as a sorcerer, your number of spells per day increases, as does the highest rank of spells you can cast.

Some of your spells require you to attempt a spell attack roll to see how effective they are, or have your enemies roll against your spell DC (typically by attempting a saving throw). Since your key ability is Charisma, your spell attack rolls and spell DCs use your Charisma modifier.

Heightening Spells

When you get spell slots of 2nd level and higher, you can fill those slots with stronger versions of lower-rank spells. This increases the spell's rank to match the spell slot. You must have a spell in your spell repertoire at the rank you want to cast in order to heighten it to that rank. Many spells have specific improvements when they are heightened to certain ranks. The signature spells class feature lets you heighten certain spells freely.

Cantrips

A cantrip is a special type of spell that doesn't use spell slots. You can cast a cantrip at will, any number of times per day. A cantrip is always automatically heightened to half your level rounded up-this is usually equal to the highest rank of spell you can cast as a sorcerer. For example, as a 1st-level sorcerer, your cantrips are 1st-rank spells, and as a 5th-level sorcerer, your cantrips are 3rd-rank spells.

feat->Bloodline.name: Bloodline
feat->Bloodline.description:
Choose a bloodline that gives you your spellcasting talent. This choice determines the type of spells you cast and the spell list you choose them from, additional spells you learn, and additional trained skills. You also gain Focus Points and special focus spells based on your bloodline. The bloodlines presented are as follows:

  • @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.classfeatures.Item.3qqvnC2U8W26yae7]{Aberrant} A strange and unknowable influence gives you occult spells.
  • @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.classfeatures.Item.vhW3glAaEfq2DKrw]{Angelic} Holy grace bestows divine spells upon you.
  • @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.classfeatures.Item.w5koctOVrEcpxTIq]{Demonic} A sinful corruption gives you divine spells.
  • @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.classfeatures.Item.o39zQMIdERWtmBSB]{Diabolic} A bond with devils gives you divine spells.
  • @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.classfeatures.Item.ZHabYxSgYK0XbjhM]{Draconic} The blood of dragons grants you arcane spells.
  • @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.classfeatures.Item.RXRnJcG4XSabZ35a]{Elemental} The power of the elements manifests in you as primal spells.
  • @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.classfeatures.Item.eW3cfCH7Wpx2vik2]{Fey} Influence from the fey gives you primal spells.
  • @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.classfeatures.Item.tYOMBiH3HbViNWwn]{Genie} Through lineage, magic, or wishes made real, the blood of a noble genie flows through your veins.
  • @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.classfeatures.Item.O0uXZRWMNliDbkxU]{Hag} The blight of a hag has given you occult spells.
  • @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.classfeatures.Item.b6hyZTs1rVGHDexz]{Harrow} You have a deep connection to the harrow.
  • @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.classfeatures.Item.ZEtJJ5UOlV5oTWWp]{Imperial} An ancient power grants you arcane spells.
  • @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.classfeatures.Item.5Wxjghw7lHuCxjZz]{Nymph} One of your ancestors was inspired by a nymph, or perhaps was a nymph themself, and now the beauty of nature lives in you.
  • @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.classfeatures.Item.TWR1wbPJuCLnGdFZ]{Phoenix} The healing fire a phoenix's blessing grants you primal spells.
  • @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.classfeatures.Item.PpzH9tJULk5ksX9w]{Psychopomp} For good or ill, your ancestors' deeds drew the attention of psychopomps, or you might somehow count one in your family tree. The presence of these shepherds of souls and enemies of undeath has left an indelible mark on you.
  • @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.classfeatures.Item.uoQOm41BVdSo6pAS]{Shadow} Whether due to a velstrac's manipulations or a planar jaunt gone horribly wrong, your bloodline was infused with a vein of shadow.
  • @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.classfeatures.Item.7WBZ2kkhZ7JorWu2]{Undead} The touch of death gives you divine spells.
  • @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.classfeatures.Item.dKTb959aCQIzSIXj]{Wyrmblessed} A draconic blessing grants you divine spells.

feat->Bloodline Spells.name: Bloodline Spells
feat->Bloodline Spells.description:
Your bloodline grants you bloodline spells, special spells unique to your lineage. Bloodline spells are a type of focus spell. It costs 1 Focus Point to cast a focus spell, and you start with a focus pool of 1 Focus Point. You refill your focus pool during your daily preparations, and you can regain 1 Focus Point by spending 10 minutes using the Refocus activity. Unlike other characters, you don't need to do anything specific to Refocus, as the power flowing through your veins naturally replenishes your focus pool.

Focus spells are automatically heightened to half your level rounded up. Focus spells don't require spell slots, nor can you cast them using spell slots. Taking feats can give you more focus spells and increase the size of your focus pool, though your focus pool can never hold more than 3 points. The full rules can be found here.

Reading A Bloodline Entry

A bloodline entry contains the following information:

Spell List You use this magical tradition and spell list.

Bloodline Skills You become trained in the listed skills.

Granted Spells You automatically add the spells listed here to your spell repertoire, as described in the Spell Repertoire class feature. At 1st level, you gain a cantrip and a 1st-rank spell. You learn the other spells on the list as soon as you gain the ability to cast sorcerer spells of that rank.

Bloodline Spells You automatically gain the initial bloodline spell at 1st level and can gain more by selecting the Advanced Bloodline and Greater Bloodline feats.

Blood Magic Whenever you cast a bloodline spell using Focus Points or a granted spell from your bloodline using a spell slot, you gain a blood magic effect. If the blood magic offers a choice, make it before resolving the spell. The blood magic effect occurs after resolving any checks for the spell's initial effects and, against a foe, applies only if the spell is a successful attack or the foe fails its saving throw. If the spell has an area, you must designate yourself or one target in the area when you cast the spell to be the target of the blood magic effect. All references to spell rank refer to the rank of the spell you cast.

feat->Bloodline: Demonic.name: Bloodline: Demonic
feat->Bloodline: Demonic.description:
Demons debase all they touch. One of your ancestors fell victim to their corruption, and you are burdened by that sin.

Spell List divine

Bloodline Skills Intimidation, Religion

Granted Spells cantrip @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.gISYsBFby1TiXfBt]{Acid Splash}, 1st: @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.4koZzrnMXhhosn0D]{Fear}, 2nd: @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.wzctak6BxOW8xvFV]{Enlarge}, 3rd: @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.WsUwpfmhKrKwoIe3]{Slow}, 4th: @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.hVU9msO9yGkxKZ3J]{Divine Wrath}, 5th: @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.vJuaxTd6q11OjGqA]{Abyssal Plague}, 6th: @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.r7ihOgKv19eJQnik]{Disintegrate}, 7th: @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.sX2o0HH4RjJDAZ8C]{Divine Decree}, 8th: @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.nsQvjNyg4Whw2mek]{Divine Aura}, 9th: @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.4WS7HrFjwNvTn8T2]{Implosion}

Bloodline Spells initial: @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.SdXFiQ4Py8761sNO]{Glutton's Jaws}, advanced: @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.iQD8OhhkwhvD8Blw]{Swamp of Sloth}, greater: @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.crF4g9jRN1y84MSD]{Chthonian Wrath}

Blood Magic The corruption of sin weakens a target's defenses or makes you more imposing. Either a target takes a -1 status penalty to AC for 1 round, or you gain a +1 status bonus to Intimidation checks for 1 round.

@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.feat-effects.Item.aKRo5TIhUtu0kyEr]{Effect: Demonic Blood Magic}

feat->Constructed (Poppet).name: Constructed (Poppet)
feat->Constructed (Poppet).description: The materials of your body resist ailments that assail the flesh. You gain a +1 circumstance bonus to saving throws against death effects, disease, and poison as well as to saving throws against effects that would give you the drained, paralyzed, or sickened conditions. Your spark of life means that you're a living creature, and you can be healed by vitality energy and harmed by void energy as normal.

0.label: Constructed (vs. disease, poisons, death effects, paralysis, drained, and sickened)

feat->Flammable.name: Flammable
feat->Flammable.description: You have weakness to fire damage equal to one-third your level (minimum 1).

feat->Familiar (Witch).name: Familiar (Witch)
feat->Familiar (Witch).description:
Your patron has sent you a familiar, a mystical creature that teaches you magic and facilitates your spells. This familiar follows the rules for familiars, though as it's a direct conduit between you and your patron, it's more powerful than typical familiars. Your familiar gains two additional familiar abilities: one of these is a unique ability based on your patron and is always selected, and the other is an additional familiar ability that can be selected daily as normal. Your familiar gains another extra ability at 6th, 12th, and 18th levels.

Spells: Your familiar is the source and repository of the spells your patron has bestowed upon you, and you must commune with your familiar to prepare your spells each day using your witch spellcasting. Your familiar starts off knowing 10 cantrips, five 1st-rank spells, and one additional spell determined by your patron's initial lesson. You choose these spells from the common spells of the tradition determined by your patron or from other spells of that tradition you gain access to. Each time you gain a level, your patron teaches your familiar two new spells of any rank for which you have spell slots, chosen from common spells of your tradition or others you gain access to. Feats can also grant your familiar additional spells.

Learning Spells: Your familiar can learn new spells independently of your patron. It can learn any spell on your tradition's spell list by physically consuming a written version of that spell over the course of 1 hour. This can be a scroll of that spell, or you can prepare a written version using the Learn a Spell exploration activity. You and your familiar can use the Learn a Spell activity to teach your familiar a spell from another witch's familiar. Both familiars must be present for the entirety of the activity, the spell must be on your spellcasting tradition's spell list, and you must pay the usual cost for that activity, typically in the form of an offering to the other familiar's patron. You can't prepare spells from another witch's familiar.

Undying: If your familiar dies, your patron replaces it during your next daily preparations. The new familiar might be a duplicate or reincarnation of your former familiar or a new entity altogether, but it knows the same spells your former familiar knew regardless. Your familiar's death doesn't affect any spells you have already prepared.

feat->Patron.name: Patron
feat->Patron.description:
You weren't born with the power to cast spells, nor have you spent years dedicated to worship, study, or uncovering mystical secrets. Your magic instead has been gifted to you by a potent being, one who knows that by empowering you so, you'll further the being's agenda in the world, whatever it may be. This entity is typically mysterious and distant, revealing little of their identity and motivations. They make their will known to you in abstract signs such as omens, dreams, or similar subtleties rather than words.

Rather than interacting with you directly, your patron works through a familiar—a mystical creature that teaches you, serves as the conduit for your patron's magic, and maybe even keeps an eye on you to ensure you're furthering your patron's nebulous ends.

At 1st level, choose your patron, which determines your spellcasting tradition and grants you the skill for that tradition of magic, your first witch's lesson and a unique familiar ability.

feat->Faith's Flamekeeper.name: Faith's Flamekeeper
feat->Faith's Flamekeeper.description:
Your patron contacted you in a moment your willpower was close to sputtering out. Their reassuring presence was like breath and kindling bringing an ember back aflame, their magic giving you the strength to carry on and bring others to your cause. Your patron is likely a divine being like an angel or aeon acting covertly, though the possibility exists they might be a more sinister entity, using you to unknown ends.

Spell List divine

Patron Skill Religion

Lesson of Fervor's Grasp Your patron has taught you that nothing can seize the heart like faith, whether used to inspire or direct. You gain the @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.GeUbPvwdZ4B4l0up]{Stoke the Heart} hex cantrip and your familiar learns @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.aIHY2DArKFweIrpf]{Command}.

Familiar of Restored Spirit Your familiar is pleasantly warm and soft, seeming to wash away worries with its mere presence. When you Cast or Sustain a hex, one willing creature within 15 feet of your familiar gains temporary Hit Points equal to 2 + half your level, which last until the start of your next turn.

feat->Witch Spellcasting.name: Witch Spellcasting
feat->Witch Spellcasting.description:
Using your familiar as a conduit, your patron provides you the power to cast spells. You are a spellcaster, and you can cast spells of your patron's tradition using the Cast a Spell activity. As a witch, when you cast spells, your incantations might rhyme, your gestures might be as simple as an ominous pointing finger or a complex folk sign, and your familiar might even echo your words or movements slightly as your patron's magic surges.

At 1st level, you can prepare up to two 1st-rank spells and five cantrips each morning from the spells your familiar knows. Prepared spells remain available to you until you cast them or until you prepare your spells again. The number of spells you can prepare each day is called your spell slots. As you increase in level as a witch, the number of spells you can prepare each day increases, as does the highest rank of spell you can cast, as shown in the Witch Spells per Day table.

Some of your spells require you to attempt a spell attack to see how effective they are, or have your enemies roll against your spell DC (typically by attempting a saving throw). Since your key attribute is Intelligence, your spell attack modifier and spell DC use your Intelligence modifier.

Heightening Spells

When you get spell slots of 2nd-rank and higher, you can fill those slots with stronger versions of lower-rank spells. This increases the spell's rank, heightening it to match the spell slot. Many spells have specific improvements when they are heightened to certain ranks.

Cantrips

Some of your spells are cantrips. A cantrip is a special type of spell that doesn't use spell slots. You can cast a cantrip at will, any number of times per day. A cantrip is always automatically heightened to half your level rounded up—this is usually equal to the highest rank of witch spell slot you have. For example, as a 1st-level witch, your cantrips are 1st-rank spells, and as a 5th-level witch, your cantrips are 3rd-rank spells.

feat->Hex Spells.name: Hex Spells
feat->Hex Spells.description:
As a favored agent of your patron, you can invoke their power for a more direct magical intervention. A hex is a spell shaped by your patron and sent to you through your familiar, rather than an ability you use directly. Your patron expects you to advance its interests and does not take kindly to repeated requests for aid; as such, you can use only one hex each turn, and any attempts to use a second hex on that turn fail and the actions are lost.

Hex spells are a type of focus spell. It costs 1 Focus Point to cast a focus spell, and you start with a focus pool of 1 Focus Point. You refill your focus pool during your daily preparations, and you can regain 1 Focus Point by spending 10 minutes using the Refocus activity to commune with your familiar.

Focus spells are automatically heightened to half your level rounded up, much like cantrips. Focus spells don't require spell slots, and you can't cast them using spell slots. Certain feats give you more focus spells. The maximum Focus Points your focus pool can hold is equal to the number of focus spells you have, but it can never be more than 3 points.

You learn your choice of the @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.aq1yonHeYpbaj3XI]{Patron's Puppet} hex or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.rMOI8JFJ0nT2mrCF]{Phase Familiar} hex, which let you command your familiar or defend it from harm, respectively. You learn most other hexes from witch lessons.

Hex Cantrips

Hex cantrips are special hexes that don't cost Focus Points, so you can cast them as often as you like, though you can still use only one hex each round. Hex cantrips are in addition to the cantrips you choose with witch spellcasting and aren't counted toward your prepared cantrips.

You gain a hex cantrip determined by your choice of patron.

consumable->Healing Potion (Minor).description:
Activate A (manipulate)

A healing potion is a vial of a ruby-red liquid that imparts a tingling sensation as the drinker's wounds heal rapidly. When you drink a healing potion, you regain [[/r 1d8[healing]]]{1d8 Hit Points}.

feat->Lesson of Protection.name: Lesson of Protection
feat->Lesson of Protection.description: You gain the @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.dFejDNEmVj3CwYLL]{Blood Ward} hex; your familiar learns @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.aAbfKn8maGjJjk2W]{Mystic Armor}.

Izni.name: Izní
Izni.appearance:
Izni trabaja duro para cultivar hongos grandes y exquisitos con el fin de proporcionar comidas sabrosas y nutritivas a sus compañeros kobolds. Mientras que, a regañadientes, entrega su parte justa de la cosecha a la tribu cuando esté lista, Izni vende sus hongos excedentes a otras criaturas subterráneas para obtener una ganancia considerable. Sueña con llegar de alguna manera a los mercados de la superficie de Absalom y comprar los excitantes y extraños hongos que comen los ciudadanos humanos.

La única criatura que parece gustarle mucho a Izni es un gato grande y esponjoso llamado Precious, que Izni rescató de un saco de arpillera. Izni afirma que Precious fue una vez heredera de la fortuna de un noble antes de que el mayordomo de la familia se deshiciera de ella para robar la herencia. Los otros kobolds no entienden del todo el afecto de Izni por un mamífero peludo, pero Izni insiste en que Precious es una pequeña criatura dracónica como los kobolds, como lo demuestran rasgos dracónicos como la arrogancia, la arrogancia y la arrogancia.carisma y rencor sin fin.

La etiqueta se reemplazará con el texto de la entrada del compendio @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.equipment-srd.Item.RjuupS9xyXDLgyIr]

@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.y6rAdMK6EFlV6U0t]{Breathe Fire}

Lanzar un hechizo desde un pergamino requiere sostener el pergamino en una mano y activarlo con una actividad de Lanzar un hechizo usando el número normal de acciones para ese hechizo.

Para lanzar un hechizo desde un pergamino, el hechizo debe aparecer en tu lista de hechizos. Como eres tú quien lanza el hechizo, usa tu tirada de ataque de hechizo ydeletrea DC. El hechizo también obtiene el rasgo apropiado para tu tradición (arcana, divina, oculta o primordial).

Todos los componentes materiales físicos y los costos se proporcionan cuando se crea un pergamino, por lo que no es necesario proporcionarlos al lanzar un hechizo a partir de un pergamino. Debes reemplazar cualquier componente material requerido para ese hechizo con un componente somático. Si el hechizo requiere un enfoque, debes tener ese enfoque para lanzar el hechizo desde un pergamino.

Nota: Para crear un pergamino o varita de un hechizo específico, arrastre el hechizo desde el compendio o navegador de compendio al inventario de un PC, NPC o actor de botín.

feat->Druidic Order.name: Druidic Order
feat->Druidic Order.description:
Upon becoming a druid, you align yourself with a druidic order, which grants you a class feat, an order spell, and an additional trained skill tied to your order. While you'll always be a member of your initial order, it's not unheard of for a druid to request to study with other orders in search of greater understanding of the natural world, and PC druids are among the most likely to blend the powers of different orders.

Order spells are a type of focus spell. It costs 1 Focus Point to cast a focus spell, and you start with a focus pool of 1 Focus Point. You refill your focus pool during your daily preparations, and you can regain 1 Focus Point by spending 10 minutes using the Refocus activity to commune with local nature spirits or otherwise tend to the wilderness in a way befitting your order.

Focus spells are automatically heightened to half your level rounded up, much like cantrips. Focus spells don't require spell slots to cast, and you can't cast them using spell slots. Selecting druid feats can give you more focus spells and increase the size of your focus pool, though your focus pool can never hold more than 3 points.

  • @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.classfeatures.Item.POBvoXifa9HaejAg]{Animal Order}
  • @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.classfeatures.Item.NdeFvIXdHwKYLiUj]{Flame Order}
  • @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.classfeatures.Item.u4nlOzPj2WHkIj9l]{Leaf Order}
  • @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.classfeatures.Item.fKTewWlYgFuhl4KA]{Stone Order}
  • @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.classfeatures.Item.acqqlYmti8D9QJi0]{Storm Order}
  • @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.classfeatures.Item.FuUXyv2yBs7zRgqT]{Wave Order}
  • @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.classfeatures.Item.v0EjtiwdeMj8ykI0]{Wild Order}

feat->Anathema (Druid).name: Anathema (Druid)
feat->Anathema (Druid).description:
As stewards of the natural order, druids find affronts to nature anathema. If you perform enough acts that are anathema to nature, you lose your magical abilities that come from the druid class, including your primal spellcasting and the benefits of your order. These abilities can be regained only if you demonstrate your repentance by conducting an @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.7Fd4lxozd11MQ55N]{Atone} ritual.

The following acts are anathema to all druids:

  • Using metal armor or shields.
  • Despoiling natural places.
  • Teaching the Druidic language to non-druids.

Each druidic order also has additional anathema acts, detailed in the order's entry.

See specific orders for more information.

feat->Druidic Language.name:
La etiqueta <Compendium> se reemplazará con el texto de la entrada del compendio @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.classfeatures.Item.RiAGlnnp4S21BAG3]

feat->Druidic Language.description: You know Druidic, a secret language known to only druids, in addition to any languages you know through your ancestry. Druidic has its own alphabet. Teaching the Druidic language to non-druids is anathema.

feat->Primal Spellcasting.name:
La etiqueta <Compendium> se reemplazará con el texto de la entrada del compendio @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.classfeatures.Item.b8pnRxGuNzG0buuh]
Lanzamiento de hechizos primordial
feat->Primal Spellcasting.description:
The power of the wild world flows through you. You can cast primal spells using the Cast a Spell activity, and you can supply material, somatic, and verbal components when casting spells. Because you're a druid, you can usually hold a primal focus (such as holly and mistletoe) for spells requiring material components instead of needing to use a material component pouch.

At 1st level, you can prepare two 1st-rank spells and five cantrips each morning from the common spells on the primal spell list, or from other primal spells to which you gain access. Prepared spells remain available to you until you cast them or until you prepare your spells again. The number of spells you can prepare is called your spell slots.

As you increase in level as a druid, the number of spells you can prepare each day increases, as does the highest rank of spell you can cast.

Some of your spells require you to attempt a spell attack roll to see how effective they are, or have your enemies roll against your spell DC (typically by attempting a saving throw). Since your key ability is Wisdom, your spell attack rolls and spell DCs use your Wisdom modifier.

feat->Shield Block.name: Shield Block
feat->Shield Block.description: You gain the @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.feats-srd.Item.jM72TjJ965jocBV8]{Shield Block} general feat, a reaction that lets you reduce damage with your shield.

feat->Wild Empathy.name:

Empatía salvaje
feat->Wild Empathy.description: You have a connection to the creatures of the natural world that allows you to communicate with them on a rudimentary level. You can use Diplomacy to Make an Impression on animals and to make very simple Requests of them. In most cases, wild animals will give you time to make your case.

feat->Animal Order.name: Animal Order
feat->Animal Order.description: You have a strong connection to beasts, and you are allied with a beast companion. You are trained in Athletics. You also gain the @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.feats-srd.Item.f2Pl5dWEL9ZvEyI1]{Animal Companion (Druid)} druid feat. You gain the @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Zmh4ynfnCtwKeAYl]{Heal Animal} order spell. Committing wanton cruelty to animals or killing animals unnecessarily is anathema to your order. (This doesn't prevent you from defending yourself against animals or killing them cleanly for food.)

You draw minor powers from your draconic exemplar. Choose a type of chromatic or metallic dragon to be your exemplar. This determines your scale color and appearance, and dragons sometimes look more favorably upon those kobolds who resemble them, at the GM's discretion. Your exemplar may also determine details of other abilities you have, using the Draconic Exemplars table.

Jadren Tagar.name: Jadren Tagar
Jadren Tagar.appearance:
Jadren ha vivido una vida en dos actos. El primer acto comenzó durante su juventud, creciendo en las calles del distrito Narrows de Oppara. Dirigió una pandilla de niños que pronto se convirtió en un gremio de carteristas y ladrones menores. Años más tarde, una redada realizada por las fuerzas del orden detuvo a la mitad de su pandilla y Jadren estaba seguro de que sus compañeros de gremio lo señalarían como el cabecilla. Huyó de la ciudad esa noche, dejando atrás el único lugar al que alguna vez había llamado hogar. Durante meses deambulóde puerto en puerto, bebiendo y divirtiéndose hasta que despertó una mañana después de haber firmado con la tripulación pirata del Brass Bird.

Al principio, no había demostrado ninguna habilidad para navegar, pero a medida que pasaron los años, aprendió casi todos los roles a bordo de un barco, desde cocinero hasta contramaestre. Jadren a menudo era el alma de todas las fiestas, contando historias extravagantes y ridículas de su juventud. Como resultado, el Capitán Renlock le tomó simpatía. Pasaron varios años buenos y elEl capitán ascendió a Jadren a primer oficial. Justo después de la promoción, todo salió mal.

Después de perseguir un barco mercante varisiano, la tripulación abordó el barco en busca de botín. Tomando todo el cargamento que pudieron encontrar, el capitán accedió a dejar ir a la tripulación y al barco, pero una bruja varisiana a bordo se ofendió por el robo y maldijo al Pájaro de Bronce y su tripulación. Ni dos días después, el barco se vio acosado por tormentas y fuertes vientos. Una semana después de eso, la marina andorana finalmente rastreóel pájaro abajo. Durante días, la tripulación de Jadren navegó hacia el sur, intentando regresar a Los Grilletes. Sin embargo, la armada andorana no cejó en su persecución. El Capitán Renlock intentó desesperadamente devolver el Brass Bird a su puerto secreto en una pequeña isla justo al norte de la nación pirata, pero los vientos estaban en su contra.

Después de una batalla campal en la que Jadren resultó gravemente herido, el Capitán Renlock se rindió. Y así, terminó el primer acto de la vida de Jadren. despertandoen el templo de Iomedae en Andoran fue lo primero que recordó después de la batalla. El sacerdote aquí atendió las heridas de Jadren y ayudó a arreglar sus huesos. Fue una recuperación larga, una que los sacerdotes querían que él hiciera por su cuenta, sin la ayuda de la magia, para que pudiera ver el costo total de la vida que había elegido. Después de semanas de curación, los sacerdotes le dieron una opción. Podría ir a prisión durante varios meses para cumplir su condena, o podría quedarse con ellos y ayudar a cuidar el templo y servir.los pobres como penitencia.

Como eso sonaba mejor que una celda de prisión, Jadren estuvo de acuerdo. Durante los siguientes meses, Jadren aprendió mucho sobre sí mismo y consideró cuidadosamente su pasado. Había vivido una vida de avaricia, pensando sólo en sí mismo y en su propio placer. Al ayudar a los demás, los sacerdotes le mostraron el valor de una vida de servicio y humildad. También le enseñaron disciplina y lo entrenaron en habilidades marciales. Las enseñanzas de estos sacerdotes también persuadieron a Jadren de que darOrientar la vida hacia un bien mayor podría proporcionar un sentido más fuerte de propósito.

Cuando llegó el momento de irse, Jadren pidió quedarse y comenzó a entrenar para convertirse en un campeón de la fe. Ahora, varios años después, Jadren se ha convertido en un hombre diferente. Es un guerrero acérrimo dedicado a Iomedae, dispuesto a dar su vida al servicio de los demás. Está especialmente interesado en ayudar a aquellos que han elegido una vida delictiva a ver el error en sus caminos como él lo ha hecho.Desafortunadamente, se da cuenta de que es demasiado tarde para su anterior amigo y mentor, el Capitán Renlock. El famoso loro mecánico del capitán, Cawlo, llegó al templo ayer mismo para informar a Jadren de su fallecimiento. Y Cawlo llegó con una petición críptica...

Metas Jadren busca expiar el daño que causó durante su vida anterior. Nada le gustaría más que cambiar su pasado, a pesar de que esto probablemente sea imposible.Aunque sabe que tal vez nunca se redima, se concentra en tomar medidas que cree que pueden mejorar el mundo.

Personalidad: Jadren es cálido y amigable, casi hasta el extremo. Si bien su comportamiento alguna vez estuvo acompañado de humor obsceno y brindis entusiastas, ahora suele ser parte de un sermón o parábola sobre Iomedae. Quienes lo conocen ahora no pueden imaginar la vida que llevaba antes. Asimismo, aquellos de una vida pasada se sorprenderían al verlo hoy.

Relaciones Aquellos de la fe aprecian profundamente a Jadren y su dedicación a las enseñanzas de Iomedae. Para los de afuera, puede parecer sermoneador y moralista.

Jaithe.name: jaith
Jaithe.appearance:
En la antigüedad, los señores de las runas de Thassilon usaban magia deformadora de carne para crear soldados horribles, principalmente aquellos conocidos hoy como engendros del pecado. Sin embargo, cuando Runelord Sorshen abandonó su dominio debajo de la actual Korvosa y le dio la espalda a su siniestro pasado, fragmentos de magia de disformidad de la piel quedaron atrás. Jaithe recuerda su tiempo en esos túneles ocultos como sueños, pero incluso entonces sabía que ella era diferente a los otros engendros del pecado. De forma demasiado humana para encajar con los monstruos deformes de carne más monstruosos,aunque de apariencia demasiado extraña para pasar por humana, interpretó el papel de una mujer misteriosa que viajó por el interior de Korvosan, al menos hasta que conoció a sus nuevos amigos. Ahora, su hogar está dondequiera que la lleven sus aventuras.

A la distancia, Jaithe parece una mujer varisiana hasta que habla, momento en el que su mandíbula inferior se bifurca como la boca de un engendro del pecado. Sus ojos son de un negro sólido, los dedos de sus manos pueden doblarse en ambos sentidos y tiene zarcillos cortos que se retuercenel interior de sus articulaciones. Cuando está en compañía mixta, Jaithe puede ocultar de alguna manera todos estos rasgos físicos cubriendo cuidadosamente su túnica ondulante.

feat->Unusual Anatomy.name: Unusual Anatomy
feat->Unusual Anatomy.description: Your unorthodox body resists physical afflictions meant for other creatures. You gain a +1 circumstance bonus to saves against diseases and poisons.

feat->Spell Repertoire (Sorcerer).name: Spell Repertoire (Sorcerer)
feat->Spell Repertoire (Sorcerer).description:
The collection of spells you can cast is called your spell repertoire. At 1st level, you learn two 1st-rank spells of your choice and four cantrips of your choice, as well as an additional spell and cantrip from your bloodline. You choose these from the common spells from the tradition corresponding to your bloodline, or from other spells from that tradition to which you have access. You can cast any spell in your spell repertoire by using a spell slot of an appropriate spell rank.

You add to this spell repertoire as you increase in level. Each time you get a spell slot, you add a spell of the same rank to your spell repertoire. At 2nd level, you select another 1st-rank spell; at 3rd level, you select two 2nd-rank spells, and so on. When you add spells, you might choose a higher-rank version of a spell you already have so that you can cast a heightened version of that spell.

Though you gain them at the same rate, your spell slots and the spells in your spell repertoire are separate. If a feat or other ability adds a spell to your spell repertoire, it wouldn't give you another spell slot, and vice versa.

Swapping Spells in your Repertoire

As you gain new spells in your spell repertoire, you might want to replace some of the spells you previously learned. Each time you gain a level and learn new spells, you can swap out one of your old spells for a different spell of the same rank. This spell can be a cantrip. You can also swap out spells by retraining during downtime.

feat->Sorcerer Spellcasting.name: Sorcerer Spellcasting
feat->Sorcerer Spellcasting.description:
Sorcerer Spellcasting

Your bloodline provides you with incredible magical power. You can cast spells using the Cast a Spell activity, and you can supply material, somatic, and verbal components when casting spells. Because you're a sorcerer, you can usually replace material components with somatic components, so you don't need to use a spell component pouch.

Each day, you can cast up to three 1st-rank spells. You must know spells to cast them, and you learn them via the spell repertoire class feature. The number of spells you can cast each day is called your spell slots.

As you increase in level as a sorcerer, your number of spells per day increases, as does the highest rank of spells you can cast.

Some of your spells require you to attempt a spell attack roll to see how effective they are, or have your enemies roll against your spell DC (typically by attempting a saving throw). Since your key ability is Charisma, your spell attack rolls and spell DCs use your Charisma modifier.

Heightening Spells

When you get spell slots of 2nd level and higher, you can fill those slots with stronger versions of lower-rank spells. This increases the spell's rank to match the spell slot. You must have a spell in your spell repertoire at the rank you want to cast in order to heighten it to that rank. Many spells have specific improvements when they are heightened to certain ranks. The signature spells class feature lets you heighten certain spells freely.

Cantrips

A cantrip is a special type of spell that doesn't use spell slots. You can cast a cantrip at will, any number of times per day. A cantrip is always automatically heightened to half your level rounded up-this is usually equal to the highest rank of spell you can cast as a sorcerer. For example, as a 1st-level sorcerer, your cantrips are 1st-rank spells, and as a 5th-level sorcerer, your cantrips are 3rd-rank spells.

feat->Bloodline.name: Bloodline
feat->Bloodline.description:
Choose a bloodline that gives you your spellcasting talent. This choice determines the type of spells you cast and the spell list you choose them from, additional spells you learn, and additional trained skills. You also gain Focus Points and special focus spells based on your bloodline. The bloodlines presented are as follows:

  • @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.classfeatures.Item.3qqvnC2U8W26yae7]{Aberrant} A strange and unknowable influence gives you occult spells.
  • @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.classfeatures.Item.vhW3glAaEfq2DKrw]{Angelic} Holy grace bestows divine spells upon you.
  • @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.classfeatures.Item.w5koctOVrEcpxTIq]{Demonic} A sinful corruption gives you divine spells.
  • @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.classfeatures.Item.o39zQMIdERWtmBSB]{Diabolic} A bond with devils gives you divine spells.
  • @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.classfeatures.Item.ZHabYxSgYK0XbjhM]{Draconic} The blood of dragons grants you arcane spells.
  • @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.classfeatures.Item.RXRnJcG4XSabZ35a]{Elemental} The power of the elements manifests in you as primal spells.
  • @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.classfeatures.Item.eW3cfCH7Wpx2vik2]{Fey} Influence from the fey gives you primal spells.
  • @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.classfeatures.Item.tYOMBiH3HbViNWwn]{Genie} Through lineage, magic, or wishes made real, the blood of a noble genie flows through your veins.
  • @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.classfeatures.Item.O0uXZRWMNliDbkxU]{Hag} The blight of a hag has given you occult spells.
  • @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.classfeatures.Item.b6hyZTs1rVGHDexz]{Harrow} You have a deep connection to the harrow.
  • @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.classfeatures.Item.ZEtJJ5UOlV5oTWWp]{Imperial} An ancient power grants you arcane spells.
  • @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.classfeatures.Item.5Wxjghw7lHuCxjZz]{Nymph} One of your ancestors was inspired by a nymph, or perhaps was a nymph themself, and now the beauty of nature lives in you.
  • @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.classfeatures.Item.TWR1wbPJuCLnGdFZ]{Phoenix} The healing fire a phoenix's blessing grants you primal spells.
  • @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.classfeatures.Item.PpzH9tJULk5ksX9w]{Psychopomp} For good or ill, your ancestors' deeds drew the attention of psychopomps, or you might somehow count one in your family tree. The presence of these shepherds of souls and enemies of undeath has left an indelible mark on you.
  • @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.classfeatures.Item.uoQOm41BVdSo6pAS]{Shadow} Whether due to a velstrac's manipulations or a planar jaunt gone horribly wrong, your bloodline was infused with a vein of shadow.
  • @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.classfeatures.Item.7WBZ2kkhZ7JorWu2]{Undead} The touch of death gives you divine spells.
  • @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.classfeatures.Item.dKTb959aCQIzSIXj]{Wyrmblessed} A draconic blessing grants you divine spells.

feat->Bloodline Spells.name: Bloodline Spells
feat->Bloodline Spells.description:
Your bloodline grants you bloodline spells, special spells unique to your lineage. Bloodline spells are a type of focus spell. It costs 1 Focus Point to cast a focus spell, and you start with a focus pool of 1 Focus Point. You refill your focus pool during your daily preparations, and you can regain 1 Focus Point by spending 10 minutes using the Refocus activity. Unlike other characters, you don't need to do anything specific to Refocus, as the power flowing through your veins naturally replenishes your focus pool.

Focus spells are automatically heightened to half your level rounded up. Focus spells don't require spell slots, nor can you cast them using spell slots. Taking feats can give you more focus spells and increase the size of your focus pool, though your focus pool can never hold more than 3 points. The full rules can be found here.

Reading A Bloodline Entry

A bloodline entry contains the following information:

Spell List You use this magical tradition and spell list.

Bloodline Skills You become trained in the listed skills.

Granted Spells You automatically add the spells listed here to your spell repertoire, as described in the Spell Repertoire class feature. At 1st level, you gain a cantrip and a 1st-rank spell. You learn the other spells on the list as soon as you gain the ability to cast sorcerer spells of that rank.

Bloodline Spells You automatically gain the initial bloodline spell at 1st level and can gain more by selecting the Advanced Bloodline and Greater Bloodline feats.

Blood Magic Whenever you cast a bloodline spell using Focus Points or a granted spell from your bloodline using a spell slot, you gain a blood magic effect. If the blood magic offers a choice, make it before resolving the spell. The blood magic effect occurs after resolving any checks for the spell's initial effects and, against a foe, applies only if the spell is a successful attack or the foe fails its saving throw. If the spell has an area, you must designate yourself or one target in the area when you cast the spell to be the target of the blood magic effect. All references to spell rank refer to the rank of the spell you cast.

feat->Signature Spells.name: Signature Spells
feat->Signature Spells.description: Experience allows you to cast some spells more flexibly. For each spell rank you have access to, choose one spell of that rank to be a signature spell. You don't need to learn heightened versions of signature spells separately; instead, you can heighten these spells freely. If you've learned a signature spell at a higher rank than its minimum, you can also cast all its lower-rank versions without learning those separately. If you swap out a signature spell, you can choose a replacement signature spell of the same spell rank at which you learned the previous spell. You can also retrain specifically to change a signature spell to a different spell of that rank without swapping any spells; this takes as much time as retraining a spell normally does.

feat->Bloodline: Aberrant.name: Bloodline: Aberrant
feat->Bloodline: Aberrant.description:
Something speaks to you from beyond the stars or below the earth. Ancient and unknowable, this alien influence presses against your mind.

Spell List occult

Bloodline Skills Intimidation, Occultism

Granted Spells cantrip @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.4gBIw4IDrSfFHik4]{Daze}, 1st: @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.DYdvMZ8G2LiSLVWw]{Spider Sting}, 2nd: @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.CQb8HtQ1BPeZmu9h]{Stupefy}, 3rd: @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.N1Z1oLPdBxaSgrEE]{Vampiric Feast}, 4th: @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.LiGbewa9pO0yjbsY]{Confusion}, 5th: @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.2w4OpAGihn1JSHFD]{Black Tentacles}, 6th: @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.5BbU1V6wGSGbrmRD]{Never Mind}, 7th: @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.8kJbiBEjMWG4VUjs]{Warp Mind}, 8th: @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Jvyy6oVIQsD34MHB]{Uncontrollable Dance}, 9th: @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Ht35SDf9PDStJfoC]{Spirit Song}

Blood Magic Aberrant whispers shield one target's mind or your own, granting a +2 status bonus to Will saving throws for 1 round.

Kaako Ashfeather.name: Pluma de ceniza de Kaako
Kaako Ashfeather.appearance:
Nacido en mares agitados por tormentas, la vida de un pirata es la única que Kaako ha conocido. Nacida bajo un signo auspicioso, los padres de Kaako la declararon una devoradora de maldiciones, alguien que podría consumir la mala suerte que caía sobre la tripulación y traerles buena suerte. Como era de esperar, a la tripulación le gustaron instantáneamente sus traviesas travesuras. Al crecer a bordo de un barco, Kaako pasó sus años de formación conociendo a todo tipo de gente del resto del mundo, navegando por las costas de Avistan yGarund. Fueron buenos años y los recuerda con cariño y nostalgia.

Años más tarde, Kaako dejó a sus padres para unirse a la tripulación del Capitán Renlock a bordo del Brass Bird. Durante algún tiempo permaneció feliz allí. La tripulación del Brass Bird se hizo un nombre como piratas a bordo de uno de los barcos más temidos que navegan por las costas. Cuando el Pájaro alcanzó un pequeño barco mercante frente a la costa de Varisia, Kaako se encontró con un maleficio que ni siquiera ella pudo evitar.Al no tener ningún deseo de tomar el barco ni los rehenes, la tripulación del Bird asaltó el barco en busca de objetos de valor antes de liberarlo. Un pasajero, una bruja varisia, se ofendió por el robo y lanzó una maldición sobre el Bird y su tripulación. Después de aquel fatídico día, el barco se vio continuamente acosado por tormentas y malos vientos. El Brass Bird constantemente chocaba con patrullas de varias armadas listas para detener el barco. Todo esto desembocó en el fatídico encuentro con la armada andorana. Perseguido de regreso a los Grilletes, el LatónBird y su tripulación finalmente fueron vencidos. Queriendo no más derramamiento de sangre, el Capitán Renlock se rindió y la tripulación del Brass Bird se dispersó.

Afortunadamente, Kaako fue liberada sólo unos meses después y volvió a vivir en mar abierto. Aunque se unió a tripulaciones a bordo de otros barcos, Kaako nunca volvió a encontrar un hogar. Cinco años después, Kaako todavía disfruta de la vida de pirata, pero se cansa de llenar los bolsillos de otras personas con oro y dejar poco para ella. EntoncesLlegó la noticia de que el Capitán Renlock había fallecido en prisión. Y su misterioso loro mecánico, Cawlo, llegó a la ventana de la cabaña de Kaako para entregar un mensaje...

Metas Kaako sueña con un futuro en el que pueda capitanear su propio barco o al menos encontrar una tripulación a la que pueda llamar familia. La vida en alta mar puede ser agotadora, especialmente cuando hay poco que mostrar después de años de trabajo.

Personalidad Kaako es curiosa, pero se asusta rápidamente. Tiene predilección por vestirse de manera extravagante, adornándose con baratijas y adornos brillantes que, según ella, la ayudan a protegerse de los malos espíritus y las maldiciones. En realidad, a ella simplemente le gusta el aspecto de estos accesorios. Kaako no está por encima de jugar con su conexión "mística", a pesar de no tener mucho control sobre esos asuntos. Sin embargo, entre amigos es mucho más relajada y puede ser bromista.

Relaciones La mayoría ve a Kaako como una persona extraña y misteriosa que tiene muchos secretos. Como resultado, pocos intentan acercarse a ella. Aquellos que llegan a conocer a la joven tengu descubren que ella puede ser una amiga cariñosa, aunque tiende a ser un poco incómoda con otros humanoides.

1.text: Si tienes éxito en una tirada de salvación contra un efecto de maldición o desgracia, obtendrás un éxito crítico.

feat->Rogue's Racket.name: Rogue's Racket
feat->Rogue's Racket.description: As you started on the path of the rogue, you began to develop your own style to pursue your illicit activities. Your racket shapes your rogue techniques and the way you approach a job, while building your reputation in the criminal underworld for a certain type of work. A client with deep pockets might even hire a team of rogues for a particular heist, each specializing in a different racket, in order to cover all the bases. Choose a rogue's racket.

feat->Sneak Attack.name: Sneak Attack
feat->Sneak Attack.description:
When your enemy can't properly defend itself, you take advantage to deal extra damage. If you Strike a creature that has the @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.AJh5ex99aV6VTggg]{Off-Guard} condition with an agile or finesse melee weapon, an agile or finesse unarmed attack, a ranged weapon attack, or a ranged unarmed attack, you deal an extra 1d6 precision damage. For a ranged attack with a thrown melee weapon, that weapon must also be agile or finesse.

As your rogue level increases, so does the number of damage dice for your sneak attack. Increase the number of dice by one at 5th, 11th, and 17th levels.

feat->Surprise Attack.name: Surprise Attack
feat->Surprise Attack.description: You spring into combat faster than foes can react. On the first round of combat, if you roll Deception or Stealth for initiative, creatures that haven't acted are off-guard to you.

feat->Deny Advantage.name: Deny Advantage
feat->Deny Advantage.description:
Barbarian Your foes struggle to pass your defenses. You aren't off-guard to hidden, undetected, or flanking creatures of your level or lower, or creatures of your level or lower using surprise attack. However, they can still help their allies flank.

Rogue As someone who takes advantage of others' defenses, you are careful not to leave such openings yourself. You aren't off-guard to hidden, undetected, or flanking creatures of your level or lower, or creatures of your level or lower using surprise attack. However, they can still help their allies flank.

feat->Weapon Tricks.name: Weapon Tricks
feat->Weapon Tricks.description: You have become thoroughly familiar with the tools of your trade. You gain expert proficiency in simple weapons as well as the @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.equipment-srd.Item.tH5GirEy7YB3ZgCk]{Rapier}, @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.equipment-srd.Item.TLQErnOpM9Luy7rL]{Sap}, @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.equipment-srd.Item.hIgqLgH3YcLZBeoT]{Shortbow}, @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.equipment-srd.Item.7tKkkF8eZ4iCLJtp]{Shortsword}, and unarmed attacks. When you critically succeed at an attack roll against an off-guard creature while using an agile or finesse simple weapon or unarmed attack, or when using any of the listed weapons, you apply the critical specialization effect for that weapon or unarmed attack.

feat->Thief.name: Thief
feat->Thief.description:
Nothing beats the thrill of taking something that belongs to someone else, especially if you can do so completely unnoticed. You might be a pickpocket working the streets, a cat burglar sneaking through windows and escaping via rooftops, or a safecracker breaking into carefully guarded vaults. You might even work as a consultant, testing clients' defenses by trying to steal something precious.

When a fight breaks out, you prefer swift, lightweight weapons, and you strike where it hurts. When you attack with a finesse melee weapon, you can add your Dexterity modifier to damage rolls instead of your Strength modifier.

You are trained in Thievery.

feat->Sharp Beak.name: Sharp Beak
feat->Sharp Beak.description: With your Sharp Beak, you are never without a weapon. You have a beak unarmed attack that deals @Damage[1d6[piercing]] damage. Your beak is in the brawling weapon group and has the finesse and unarmed traits.

feat->Reactive Strike.name: Reactive Strike
feat->Reactive Strike.description: Ever watchful for weaknesses, you can quickly attack foes that leave an opening in their defenses. You gain the @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.KAVf7AmRnbCAHrkT]{Reactive Strike} reaction.

feat->Bravery.name: Bravery
feat->Bravery.description: Having faced countless foes and the chaos of battle, you have learned how to stand strong in the face of fear. Your proficiency rank for Will saves increases to expert. When you roll a success at a Will save against a fear effect, you get a critical success instead. In addition, anytime you gain the @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.TBSHQspnbcqxsmjL]{Frightened} condition, reduce its value by 1.

Choose one weapon group. Your proficiency rank increases to master with the simple weapons, martial weapons, and unarmed attacks in that group, and to expert with the advanced weapons in that group. You gain access to the critical specialization effects of all weapons and unarmed attacks for which you have master proficiency.

Kellsti.name: Kellsti
Kellsti.appearance:
Kellsti escuchó por primera vez de la promesa de Nocticula de brindar refugio a los artistas asediados por medio de un clérigo después de su rescate de una banda de cazarrecompensas en Bloodsworn Vale. Durante un tiempo, acompañó a la sacerdotisa como acólita, pero se vio obligada a actuar por su cuenta después de que un oso búho se comiera a su patrón. Kellsti espera algún día llegar a las alturas de la montañosa Nueva Thassilon, donde ha oído que se reúnen los adoradores de Nocticula, pero quiere causar una buena impresión. Primero, ellaquiere crear una obra maestra inspirada en sus experiencias de vida. Originalmente, Korvosa parecía ser el lugar perfecto para encontrar este tipo de experiencias, pero después de conocer a sus nuevos amigos y viajar con ellos, se dio cuenta de que son mucho más inspiradoras.

Kellsti es un kobold de escamas de bronce con una línea de plumas blancas y rosadas que le recorre la espalda y la cola. Sus brillantes ojos verdes brillan suavemente y cuando habla, lo hace con una voz casi cantarina.

heritage->Ganzi.name: Ganzi
heritage->Ganzi.description: Your blood is touched by primal chaos. You gain the ganzi trait in addition to the traits from your ancestry. You gain resistance to a single damage type equal to half your level; at the beginning of each day, determine randomly whether this resistance applies to acid, electricity, or sonic damage. You also gain a +1 circumstance bonus to saving throws against effects that would cause you to gain the @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.9qGBRpbX9NEwtAAr]{Controlled} condition. You can choose from ganzi feats and feats from your ancestry whenever you gain an ancestry feat.

Puedes otorgar los puntos de vida que recuperarías de @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.feats-srd.Item.yExxOkHN1PN37hUa]{Communal Healing} a cualquier criatura dentro del alcance de tu hechizo de curación en lugar de a ti mismo. También puedes usar curación comunitaria cuando te diriges solo a ti mismo con un hechizo curar , aunque si lo haces, debes otorgar la curación adicional a alguien que no seas tú mismo.

consumable->Wand of Heal (Level 1).name:
